steps.title: Find the original center
steps.content: The equation $(x-6)^2+(y+5)^2=4$ is in circle form, so circle P has center $(6,-5)$ and radius $2$.

steps.title: Translate the center
steps.content:
Moving right $3$ units adds $3$ to the $x$-coordinate and leaves the $y$-coordinate unchanged:
$$
(6,-5)\to(9,-5)
$$

steps.title: Write the new equation
steps.content: With center $(9,-5)$ and the same radius, circle Q has equation: $$ (x-9)^2+(y+5)^2=4 $$ The answer is C.
